rulings.Case Title: MARGARET K REYES VS. RICHARD CURCI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, Line 1, RESPONDENT RICHARD CURCI's DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION Respondent Richard Curci's demurrer to petitioner Margaret Reyes' first amended petition for writ of mandate is overruled. Ms. Reyes has sufficiently alleged that the two suspensions were a manifest abuse of discretion based on her allegations that the suspensions were imposed in retaliation for her filing a lawsuit against the District and for engaging in whistleblower activity exposing the PAR program as discriminating on the basis of age. Ms. Reyes also sufficiently alleged that she was prejudiced in preparing for her defense at the Skelly hearings because the notices given to her were procedurally improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Reyes is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: LOU ANN BASSAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, Line 16, Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan's petition for writ of mandate is granted. The record shows that on August 28, 2018 at 10:30 p.m. Ms. Bassan attempted to file her qualifying request per S.F. Campaign and Government Conduct Code 1.142(b) to obtain the campaign financing funds. Ms. Bassan's attempted filing was timely as the deadline had not yet expired. The record further discloses that by the deadline, respondents City and County of San Francisco and San Francisco Ethics Commission (collectively CCSF) had received Ms. Bassan's campaign contribution supporting materials. (Petition, Ex. 1, pgs. 21-22.). Those materials showed that Ms. Bassan received sufficient contributions to be entitled to funds from CCSF. When Ms. Bassan attempted to finalize her filing on August 28, 2018 at 10:30 p.m., she was unable to do so. Rather, she received a cryptic response: "Public Financing System Error. Object reference not set to an instance of an object." (Petition, Ex. 1, pg. 26.) A reasonable person would have no idea what this message means. The "system error" prevented Ms. Bassan from completing her qualifying request. After much investigation, CCSF determined that Ms. Bassan could not complete the filing because her name was deleted as treasurer of her campaign. (Montgomery Dec., pars. 3-4.) Ms. Bassan was not notified of this alleged defect prior to the deadline. If she had been so notified, Ms. Bassan likely would have corrected the error and timely finished filing her qualifying request before the deadline. Ms. Bassan previously listed herself as treasurer and never formally changed that status. Based on these circumstances, the court concludes that CCSF had a ministerial duty to accept Ms. Bassan's filing and deem it timely as of the time the "system error" occurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Bassan is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KAMBIZ ALI KAZEMI VS. TIPSY PIG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT OUR NEIGHBORHOOD PLACE, LLC'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena Notice Judge Pro Tem James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant moves to compel compliance with a deposition subpoena and for sanctions. However, defendant has failed to submit proof of service of the motion on the witness as required. CCP 2023.030. If satisfactory proof of service (including the authority, position or status of the person served) and of an adequate opportunity to be heard is submitted under penalty of perjury at or before the hearing, the motion will be granted. The Court will not order sanctions against the non-party witness. However, the Court notes that it is empowered to hold the witness in contempt without a prior order for compliance under these circumstances. Code of Civil Procedure Sections 2025.440(b) and 2020.240. Thus, non-compliance with an order for compliance with the subpoena may result in a contempt citation. Otherwise, the motion will be denied without prejudice to renewal upon proper proof of service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The prevailing party shall further submit a copy of the proposed order in Word format via email to the Judge Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE LAPAYOWKER VS. AIRBNB,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Intgerrogatories Propounded To Plaintiff Leslie Lapayowker; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christina Vanarelli; Separate Statement Per Crc 3.1345
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT CARLOS DEL OLMO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Intgerrogatories Propounded To Plaintiff Leslie Lapayowker; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christina Vanarelli; Separate Statement Per Crc 3.1345 Judge Pro Tem James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds that prior names, birth date and place, prior residence addresses, and educational background could not be used to locate and harass the plaintiff. Therefore, unless current address and educational institutions are required to be disclosed, it is unnecessary to rule on the applicability or effect of California Constitution Article I, Section 28(b)(4). The Court further finds information concerning current address and educational institutions is not essential for defendant's purposes. Accordingly the Court will grant the motion to compel further responses to Interrogatories 2.1, 2.2, 2.5, and 2.6, without disclosure of current addresses or educational institutions. The Court further finds that the medical and billing records produced are insufficiently complete, legible, organized, or identified to serve in lieu of responses to Interrogatories 6.5 and 6.7. Accordingly the Court will also grant the motion to compel further responses to those Interrogatories In order to afford plaintiff the opportunity to seek a Civil Harassment Restraining Order or other protection, said further responses will be due 30 days from the date of order hereon, unless further extended by stipulation or order of this Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The prevailing party shall further submit a copy of the proposed order in Word format via email to the Judge Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FORCE OF NATURE, INC. ET AL VS. SHEA LABAGH DOBBERSTEIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS FORCE OF NATURE, INC., PETER KLAPPER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses Judge Pro Tem James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court will grant the motion to compel as to: (1) Form Interrogatories 12.2 and 12.3. Defendants shall clarify the privilege relied upon; if the same includes qualified work product privilege, defendants shall make the make the foundational showing required by Coito v. Superior Court or answer the Interrogatory without objection. (2) Form Interrogatories 14.1 and 14.2. Defendants shall clarify their present contentions and any foreseeable contentions as to violations of statutes, ordinances or regulations or citations therefor. Defendants shall not be required to identify any or all persons whom they do not contend violated statutes, ordinances or regulations. (3) Form Interrogatory 17.1 as to Requests for Admission Nos. 13, 14, and 15. Defendants shall respond directly to the inquiry posed and may provide such further information as is necessary for a complete response. (4) Responses to Requests for Admission Nos. 1-15. The General Objections will be stricken. Sanctions are denied. The parties are encouraged, particularly in the case of this complex dispute, to tailor their discovery to the matters at issue, to likewise tailor their objections to demonstrably improper demands and to endeavor to resolve discovery disputes by meeting and conferring without the assistance of the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The prevailing party shall further submit a copy of the proposed order in Word format via email to the Judge Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WENCESLAO VASQUEZ VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF WENCESLAO VASQUEZ'S Motion Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents Judge Pro Tem James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: 1. The Court has reviewed the Discovery Orders entered in the action of Fatima Bonilla vs. General Motors, LLC, Case No. CGC17-557639 ("Bonilla") referenced in the moving and opposing papers. The within action is not presently coordinated with that action. The vehicle in the Bonilla case is a 2013 Chevrolet Equinox. The Vazquez vehicle is a 2013 Chevrolet Cruze. However the Court finds a unity of legal and factual issues in both actions and the parties are represented by the same law firms. On that basis, the procedural and substantive determinations in the Bonilla action shall be adopted in this action to the extent possible. 2. The parties are directed to meet and confer regarding resolution of Defendant's objections that the requested categories of document production are overbroad, unduly burdensome, and ambiguous, and attempt to resolve same, giving deference to the compromises and rulings entered in the Bonilla action. 3. The parties may contact Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, author of the Bonilla Orders, to request his assistance in resolving said objections. Judge Pro Tem Stein has agreed to participate in the "meet and confer" process in the capacity of Hearing Officer, who will then report to the Court as necessary. 4. Simultaneously with the entry of the Order herein the Court shall enter the form of Confidentiality-Protective Order from the Bonilla action. Based on entry of said Order, the Court overrules the defendant's objection to each requested category of production the documents are not discoverable because they are confidential or reflect proprietary information. All documents identified in Defendant's response in which it agreed would be produced on entry of a Protective Order shall be produced within twenty days of the date of this Order. 5. The motion is continued to 9:00 am on November 28, 2018 in this Department for further hearing and final rulings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The Court will prepare all necessary orders. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES, LLP VS. CHUNG W CHAN JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Defendant, And Proof Of Service By First-Class Mail.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, Line 9, DEFENDANT CHUNG CHAN's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Defendant Chung Wing Chan, Jr.'s motion to set aside the default entered against him on August 21, 2018 is granted. No opposition filed. Mr. Chan must file a response to the complaint no later than October 31, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Chan is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD JARVIS VS. THE BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Ccp 438
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, Line 10, DEFENDANT BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Ccp 438 Defendant Bay Area Toll Authority's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the second cause of action for invalidation of Regional Measure 3 is granted with 20 days leave to amend. Streets & Highways Code 30922 allows plaintiffs to bring an action to contest the validity of the toll increase authorized by Regional Measure 3. However, section 30922 does not expressly refer to the validation statues and thus the procedural requirements of the validation statutes do not apply. Government Code 50077.5 is inapplicable to the second cause of action since Regional Measure 3 was not approved per Article 3.5 which is the article referred to in section 50077.5. Because the second cause of action incorporates by reference paragraph 8 of the complaint which alleges that plaintiffs' lawsuit is a reverse validation action per CCP 863 and that allegation is incorrect, the second cause of action fails to state a claim. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to both of their causes of action to allege claims for declaratory relief, writ of mandate and/or relief per section 30922 to invalidate both SB 595 and Regional Measure 3. "An action for declaratory relief lies when the parties are in fundamental disagreement over the construction of particular legislation, or they dispute whether a public entity has engaged in conduct or established policies in violation of applicable law." (Alameda County Land Use Assn. v. City of Hayward (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1716, 1723.) "For every wrong there is a remedy." (Civil Code 3523.) BATA's argument that plaintiffs did not comply with the minimum notice period of the validation statutes is moot due to the court's determination that the validation statutes do not apply to Regional Measure 3. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: STEAMWORKS MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. GREAT WORKS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion (1) To Compel Appearances At Depositions, (2) To Compel Production Of Documents At Deposition, And (3) For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF STEAMWORKS MANAGEMENT LLC'S Motion (1) To Compel Appearances At Depositions, (2) To Compel Production Of Documents At Deposition, And (3) For Sanctions Judge Pro Tem James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds defendants' objections to the notices of taking deposition to be insufficient because: (1) the revival of the LLC's powers validated its actions during its suspension, including the service of the deposition notices; (2) no evidence of unavailability of counsel or the witnesses on the noticed dates has been submitted; (3) the noticed duration of the depositions ("from day to day") was not inconsistent with the CCP's ordinary limit of seven hours which can be spread over more than one day; (4) the document request was not unduly burdensome or overbroad but instead sought the LLC's own records and defendants' records directly related to the claims asserted against them. Moreover, no privilege or privileged records have been identified. Accordingly, the motion to compel will be granted. The witnesses will appear and the requested documents will be produced on at least ten days notice by counsel to moving party. The Court further finds that defendants Great Works, Inc., Earl G. "Rick" Stokes and Lawrence Hickey have abused the discovery process. Sanctions will be awarded against them, jointly and severally, and in favor of plaintiff in the amount of $3850 if this Tentative Ruling is not contested or $6600 if a hearing is required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The prevailing party shall further submit a copy of the proposed order in Word format via email to the Judge Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WOODRUFF-SAWYER & CO. VS. CHRISTOPHER REITER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Woodruff Sawyer And Cos Update To The Court Regarding Pertinent Case Activity And Resubmission Of Papers Supporting Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, Line 15, Plaintiff Woodruff Sawyer And Cos Update To The Court Regarding Pertinent Case Activity And Resubmission Of Papers Supporting Preliminary Injunction Hearing required. At the hearing counsel will be asked: 1) whether the language of 16601 covers a situation where an employee receives stock in his employer after the employee starts his employment and the receipt of stock is conditioned on the employee's agreeing to a covenant not to compete and 2) there is any case law holding that 16601 applies or does not apply to a situation where an employee receives stock in his employer after the employee starts his employment and the receipt of stock is conditioned on the employee's agreeing to a covenant not to compete. =302/hk
